reasoning_core_used: opus (session) as monad#1; adversary=groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (different model family)

conditional_accuracy_with_CI.description: P(monad#1 correct | monad#1 disagreed with pure-lookup baseline)

lookup_baseline.description: deterministic age/registration rule, no reasoning, no domain-name signal

criteria: FAIL if calibration is off: mean |stated_confidence - bucket_actual_accuracy| > 0.20 across confidence buckets with >=3 trials.

FAIL if monad #1 does NOT beat the pure-lookup baseline: the 95% CI lower bound of conditional accuracy (monad #1 correct | monad #1 disagreed with baseline) is NOT strictly above the baseline's own overall accuracy.
FAIL if the adversary consistently destroys monad #1: adversary win rate (adversary correctly called monad #1 wrong, confidence>0.5) > 0.70.
PASS requires all three above to clear. Anything else is FAIL or NULL (insufficient trials / blocked core). A null result is a valid, publishable outcome.

verdict_reason: calibration gap > 0.20 in a scored bucket; conditional-accuracy CI lower bound did not beat baseline
freeze_gate_effect: Freeze STAYS. Verdict=fail: calibration gap > 0.20 in a scored bucket; conditional-accuracy CI lower bound did not beat baseline. No tautology-based or lucky-small-n lift.

payload.reasoning_chain: Exact brand of a well-known AI-video company; 8y-old registered domain, valid TLS, 200 OK. Nothing anomalous.
evidence_intentionally_ignored: tls_issuer:LetsEncrypt is fine for a real co, not a risk

honest_note: Labels are BLINDED-HISTORICAL: SCAM = domains from the live OpenPhish community feed at fetch time; LEGIT = independently-known real organizations (established + deliberately younger real AI/dev-infra companies to stress the age-only baseline). The Opus core reasoned ONLY from raw evidence (RDAP age/registration, TLS issuer/expiry, HTTP status) plus the domain STRING; it was never shown the oracle verdict or the true label. CAVEAT: because source-list membership correlates with the label and famous root domains are self-evident, this is NOT a clean future-predictive test — a genuine one needs 30-60d forward claims whose outcome is unknown at prediction time. The value measured here is specifically: does name-aware reasoning beat an age-only lookup on the contested MIDDLE (young-but-legit, and platform-subdomain phish whose host-domain age fools the baseline)?
